description: Fix two bugs in the Stripe plugin: (1) api() uses raw fetch() with no AbortSignal, so requests can hang indefinitely and the AbortError catch branch is unreachable dead code; (2) get-account.ts throws a plain Error instead of ToolError.internal.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/stripe && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add AbortSignal.timeout to api() and fix dead AbortError catch
userStories.description: In plugins/stripe/src/stripe-api.ts, the api() function calls raw fetch() at line 84 with no signal in the RequestInit object, so requests can hang indefinitely if Stripe's server is unresponsive. The catch block at line 86 checks for AbortError but this branch is unreachable since no AbortSignal is provided. Add AbortSignal.timeout(30_000) to the RequestInit and replace the dead AbortError check with a TimeoutError check.
acceptanceCriteria: The RequestInit object passed to fetch() in api() includes signal: AbortSignal.timeout(30_000)
The catch block checks for TimeoutError (err.name === 'TimeoutError') not AbortError
The timeout error throws ToolError.timeout() with a descriptive message
No AbortError check remains in the catch block (it was dead code with no abort signal)

userStories.title: Replace throw new Error() with ToolError.internal() in get-account.ts
userStories.description: In plugins/stripe/src/tools/get-account.ts line 26, `throw new Error('Account data not available')` throws a plain Error that bypasses the ToolError dispatch chain, losing the error category and retryability fields. Replace with ToolError.internal().
acceptanceCriteria: Line 26 in get-account.ts uses ToolError.internal('Account data not available') instead of new Error()
No plain Error throws remain in get-account.ts
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/stripe/src/tools/get-account.ts (line 26)

Context:
Current line 26:
  if (!merchant) throw new Error('Account data not available');

Change to:
  if (!merchant) throw ToolError.internal('Account data not available');

Make sure ToolError is imported from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk'. The import already exists (check the file imports — if not present, add it).
